title: Infer hidden cheap-stop and crossover regimes from field-synchronous shocks
summary: Public F1 analysis repeatedly shows pit timing changes abruptly when SC/VSC makes stops cheaper or when wet/dry crossover points are reached.
rationale: Public F1 analysis repeatedly shows pit timing changes abruptly when SC/VSC makes stops cheaper or when wet/dry crossover points are reached. Your table has no explicit caution or weather flag, so the useful signal is likely in race-lap-wide synchronous anomalies rather than in single-row tyre features.
implementation_hint: Build causal race-lap aggregates such as mean/std of `LapTime_Delta`, share of drivers with extreme positive deltas, wet-tyre share, compound-switch counts, and field compression proxies. Fit a small HMM/clusterer or direct classifier out-of-fold to create 3-5 latent regime states, then feed the current state and recent state transitions into the main model or a specialist expert.
expected_effect: Should improve recall on opportunistic pits that static finish-window rules miss, especially around VSC/SC-like laps and wet/dry transition laps.
risk: Unsupervised states can be unstable and leak if fit on full data. Learn states fold-pure by held-out race groups and only from current/prior race laps.
